trigger_reason: R787 is rank by mechanical-PASS proximity in E32 (post-filter architectural candidate with kw=2; tree-stream max sim=0.40). Per program_v10.md §2.5 (preserved in v11/v12/v13), top-3 rounds receive full step-13 spec.
candidate_stripped: Crystal-basis attention layer: new attention layer whose key/query weights are constrained to a Kashiwara crystal basis of an irreducible highest-weight module; attention weights are computed via combinatorial monomial paths over the crystal, replacing softmax with a tableaux-indexed transition function.

dataset.name: Long Range Arena (LRA: Pathfinder, ListOps) + WMT16 En-De

A_candidate.name: Crystal-basis attention layer
A_candidate.description: Replace softmax attention with a tableaux-indexed transition function where keys/queries are constrained to Kashiwara crystal basis vectors of an irreducible sl_n+1 highest-weight module. Attention 'weights' are computed via combinatorial monomial paths over the crystal graph rather than dot-product + softmax. The crystal-basis structure introduces a combinatorial sparsity (only adjacent crystal edges have non-zero attention).
A_candidate.distinctive_parameter: new crystal-basis vectors + learnable tableau-transition coefficients

B_control.name: Same architecture WITH standard softmax attention

metric.name: LRA Pathfinder accuracy + WMT16 BLEU + sparsity pattern (Hamming-distance between learned attention and crystal-basis-implied sparsity)

statistical_test.name: paired t-test A vs B + A vs C + sparsity-pattern correlation analysis

distinguishability_pre_check.rationale: Crystal basis is a discrete algebraic structure on the highest-weight representation; the attention pattern induced by tableau transitions is combinatorially distinct from softmax (softmax is dense; crystal-basis transitions are sparse and structured by the weight lattice). The combinatorial sparsity pattern of A is empirically distinguishable from B (softmax dense) and C (LSH random sparse). The attention pattern's Hamming-distance from crystal-basis sparsity is a direct measurable distinguishability metric.
distinguishability_pre_check.v12_observation: Step 05.5 PASS = architectural-topology-change; candidate has new learnable module with new crystal-basis vectors and tableau-transition coefficients. The combinatorial structure of crystal basis is genuinely different from softmax attention's continuous distribution.

notes_for_executor: R787's architectural-topology candidate replaces softmax with crystal-basis transition function; the sparsity pattern is structurally distinct from both dense softmax and random LSH-sparse.
